name: Header
description: Checks that a source file begins with a specified header. Property "headerFile" specifies a file that contains the required header. Alternatively, the header specification can be set directly in the "header" property without the need for an external file. In default configuration, if header is not specified, the default value of header is set to "null" and the check does not rise any violations.

options.name: charset
options.description: Specify the character encoding to use when reading the headerFile.

options.name: fileExtensions
options.description: Specify the file extensions of the files to process.

options.name: header
options.description: Specify the required header specified inline. Individual header lines must be separated by the string "\n"(even on platforms with a different line separator).

options.name: headerFile
options.description: Specify the name of the file containing the required header.

options.name: ignoreLines
options.description:
Specifies the line numbers
 to ignore when matching lines in a content of headerFile.

name: MultiFileRegexpHeader
description: Checks the header of a source file against multiple header files that contain a pattern for each line of the source header.

options.name: fileExtensions
options.description: Specify the file extensions of the files to process.

options.name: headerFiles
options.description:
Specify a comma-separated list of files containing the required headers.
 If a file's header matches none, the violation references
 the first file in this list. Users can order files to set
 a preferred header for such reporting.

name: RegexpHeader
description: Checks the header of a source file against a header that contains a pattern for each line of the source header.

options.name: charset
options.description: Specify the character encoding to use when reading the headerFile.

options.name: fileExtensions
options.description: Specify the file extensions of the files to process.

options.name: header
options.description: Define the required header specified inline. Individual header lines must be separated by the string "\n" (even on platforms with a different line separator). For header lines containing "\n\n" checkstyle will forcefully expect an empty line to exist. See examples below. Regular expressions must not span multiple lines.

options.name: headerFile
options.description: Specify the name of the file containing the required header.

options.name: multiLines
options.description: Specify the line numbers to repeat (zero or more times).
